query: When is overpressure protection required on a distribution line?
unsupported_claims: 49 CFR 192.195 requires distribution systems supplied from a high-pressure source to have pressure-relieving or pressure-limiting devices. | Overpressure protection is required at points where gas is reduced from a higher-pressure source into a lower-pressure distribution system. | 49 CFR 192.197 covers the design and capacity of pressure-relieving and pressure-limiting stations. | 49 CFR 192.199 addresses requirements for pressure-limiting and regulating stations.

query: Do residential service lines need excess flow valves?
unsupported_claims: EFVs are required on small commercial customers with a gas load not exceeding 1,000 SCFH. | A customer’s gas load may cause nuisance trips, making an EFV impractical.

query: What’s the maximum operating pressure allowed for plastic pipe?
unsupported_claims: Plastic pipe may not be operated above the pressure that produces a hoop stress of 100% of the pipe’s Hydrostatic Design Basis (HDB). | Plastic pipe may not be operated above the manufacturer’s listed pressure rating. | The maximum operating pressure for plastic pipe is determined using a formula in 192.121. | The formula in 192.121 is based on the pipe’s outside diameter and wall thickness. | The maximum operating pressure cannot exceed the manufacturer’s rating.

query: Is pressure testing required for all new distribution pipelines?”
unsupported_claims: For new distribution pipelines, an operator may use a leak survey with leakage repairs under 49 CFR 192.281(c). | A leak survey and repair process may be used instead of pressure testing if specific conditions are met.

query: For a distribution system operating above 60 psig, what kind of overpressure protection is required?
unsupported_claims: This requirement is specified in 49 CFR 192.195. | Subpart D addresses overpressure protection provisions for distribution systems.
